channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from multiple sources

item.title: China Confirms Boeing Aircraft Purchase Deal Following Trump Visit
item.description: Chinese Ministry of Commerce confirms Beijing will buy at least 200 Boeing aircraft and engines, marking a significant trade concession following Trump's summit with Xi.

item.title: Pakistan Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Talks With Iranian Officials
item.description: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visits Tehran amid regional tensions, as Pakistan navigates relations with Iran during the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: Putin to Visit Beijing Days After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet with Xi Jinping on a two-day trip to Beijing next week, signaling continued Russia-China coordination following Trump's visit.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Aircraft Deep Behind Enemy Lines
item.description: Zelensky releases footage of Ukrainian strikes on Russian military targets including a Be-200 amphibious aircraft, demonstrating continued long-range strike capabilities.

item.title: Moscow Signals Frustration as US-Russia Contacts Remain Difficult
item.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Ryabkov indicates that US-Russia diplomatic contacts remain slow and difficult amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Hits Wall With Iran
item.description: After a year of concessions on tariffs and conflicts, U.S. President Donald Trump's confrontational negotiating style has met its match in Iran, where neither threats nor military action have produced the desired outcome.

item.title: Trump Returns From China With Stability and Stalemate
item.description: President Donald Trump's Beijing summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping produced modest results but highlighted China's desire for stability after last year's trade war extremes, with the US-China relationship now defined by managed competition.

item.title: Ukraine Rescue Teams Pull Bodies From Kyiv Flats After Massive Russian Strikes
item.description: At least seven people including a girl were killed in Kyiv as Russia launched its largest drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, with rescuers searching through rubble for survivors amid intense bombardment.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days to enable further diplomatic progress, the U.S. State Department announced, even as Israel continues strikes it says fall outside the truce agreement.

item.title: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in Joint US-Nigeria Operation
item.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint operation by US and Nigerian forces in northeastern Nigeria. Both presidents Trump and Tinubu confirmed the elimination of the terrorist leader.

item.title: Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO From Belarus
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy stated that Russia is considering plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory, drawing Belarus deeper into the war.

item.title: US Accuses Iraqi Man of Helping Iran-Backed Militia Plot Attacks in US and Europe
item.description: The US Justice Department announced the arrest of an Iraqi national accused of involvement in multiple attacks against US interests in Europe and planning attacks on US soil for an Iran-backed militia.

item.title: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Countries After Trump Greenland Threats
item.description: Following Trump's threats to seize Greenland, Canadian authorities have been strengthening defense cooperation with Nordic countries. Greenland has been seeking support from its northern ally Canada.

item.title: Taiwan Presses US for Arms Sales After Trump Undecided on Future Deliveries
item.description: Taiwan's government emphasized that US arms sales are essential for regional peace and deterrence, following President Trump's statement that he has not decided on future weapons sales to the island.

item.title: Council of Europe Approves Special Tribunal to Try Russian Leadership for Aggression
item.description: The Council of Europe has approved steps to establish a Nuremberg-style tribunal to prosecute Russia's leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine. 36 countries and the EU have committed to supporting the Special Tribunal.

item.title: GOP Rep. Massie Introduces AIPAC Act Targeting Foreign Lobbying Days Before Primary
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie, the harshest Republican critic of Israel in Congress, introduced legislation aimed at foreign lobbying efforts just days before his Kentucky primary. The bill targets AIPAC and similar organizations.

item.title: Putin to Visit Beijing May 20 for Talks with Xi, Days After Trump Summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will visit Beijing on May 20 for a one-day trip, the South China Morning Post reported. The visit comes days after Xi's summit with Trump and is seen as routine coordination between the strategic partners.

item.title: Turkish Prosecutors Expand Istanbul Corruption Investigation Targeting Opposition Municipality
item.description: Turkish police detained 12 suspects as part of an expanding corruption investigation into alleged irregularities in municipal tenders linked to Istanbul's opposition-run metropolitan municipality. The probe has intensified political tensions.

item.title: Senior Islamic State leader killed in US-Nigeria joint operation
item.description: US and Nigerian forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described by President Trump as ISIS's second-in-command globally, in a precision strike on his compound in the Lake Chad Basin. Nigerian President Tinubu called it the most consequential counterterrorism outcome since regional operations began in 2015.

item.title: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue despite ceasefire extension
item.description: Despite Israel and Lebanon agreeing to extend their ceasefire for 45 more days, Israeli warplanes continued striking villages in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah opposes the negotiations as Israeli forces maintain strikes and occupation in parts of the south.

item.title: Hamas military chief dies after Israeli airstrike
item.description: A senior Hamas official confirmed the death of the groups military wing commander, a day after Israel announced it had carried out airstrikes targeting him. This marks a significant blow to Hamas leadership.

item.title: Russia captures two villages in Ukraines Kharkiv region
item.description: Russian forces have taken control of Borova and Kutkivka settlements in Kharkiv, according to Russias Defence Ministry cited by RIA. The advance comes amid continued fighting in eastern Ukraine.

item.title: UK Prime Minister to approve 24 billion dollar defense spending increase
item.description: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to approve an 18 billion pound defense spending boost as he faces political challenges. The increase comes amid broader European security concerns and NATO commitments.

item.title: UK Markets Slide on Political Turmoil and Oil Price Fears
item.description: British equities and bonds fell as investors weighed a potential leadership challenge to PM Keir Starmer alongside mounting concerns about oil-driven inflation from the Iran-Hormuz crisis.

item.title: Trump Warns Taiwan: No Blank Check from US Military
item.description: President Trump said Taiwan should not expect a blank check from the US military and stated he is not looking to have someone go independent, in comments about the Taiwan question.
